description: Lock direct-source scope, resolved chronology, and removal of topic-as-prerequisite edges across the second trust-remediation batch.

checks.reason: The Duvensee paddle does not identify the construction type of its associated boat.
The scoped pine artifact requires shaping wood into a blade and shaft.
The artifact remains anchored to the museum's 6200 BCE date.
The scoped curd separation requires a managed animal-milk supply.
The direct evidence is milk residue in purpose-made perforated pottery strainers.
The cheese-straining evidence remains anchored within the sixth millennium BCE.
Volley drill does not require print production.
Coordinated group volleys require organized infantry ranks.
Gunpowder firearms define the scoped musketry formation.
The source-backed scope remains seventeenth-century musket volley formations.
The generic organization edge is inherited through the specific musketry path.
Ancient iron production is not the direct tactical dependency of pike and shot.
The mixed formation necessarily includes ranked musketeers protected by pikes.
The scope remains anchored to the circa 1620-1630 museum object and tactic description.
Assay work is a recipe subject, not infrastructure for the printed genre.
Dyeing is optional recipe content, not a publication prerequisite.
The scoped Books of Secrets are a popular printed-book genre.
The node remains anchored to the documented octavo Thesaurus Pauperum edition.
The 1449 English grant predates the later Venetian printing privilege.
The legal grant tradition predates and does not require scientific method.
The 1449 grant tradition predates the later joint-stock company node.
English and Welsh invention-patent records remain anchored to 1449.
The 1711 detailed written specification remains distinct from later patent drawings.
Later Patent Office publication is not the original enrolled disclosure process.
A patent specification exists within the patent-grant process it supplements.
The first detailed British patent specification remains anchored to 1711.
A printing privilege is not constitutive of the surviving 1594 quarto.
The printed artifact reproduces a play performed by named acting companies.
Printed sheets are constitutive of the quarto artifact.
The play-quarto scope remains anchored to the 1594 Titus Andronicus edition.
Education was a reform demand rather than a prerequisite of the convention.
Print was one campaign channel among several organizing strategies.
Industrial city growth is not a documented prerequisite of Seneca Falls.
Earlier equality and individual-rights arguments supply the movement's intellectual lineage.
The organized U.S. suffrage scope remains anchored to Seneca Falls in 1848.
The 1840s newsroom service predates the repository's 1863 ticker system.
Domestic newsrooms predate the repository's 1866 transoceanic agency node.
The scoped service receives remote information over electric telegraph lines.
The telegraphic-newsroom scope remains anchored to the 1840s museum account.
